GeoRAG Test Fixtures — Files Created
======================================
Date: 2026-04-10
Project: /home/Development/GeoRAG Intelligence V.1.0

DOCUMENTATION FILES (6 files)
=============================

1. tests/fixtures/README.md
   - Quick start guide for all fixtures
   - Overview of each fixture (Excel, SEG-Y, XYZ)
   - Generation methods (master generator, individual, Docker)
   - Dagster integration instructions
   - Validation procedures
   - Dependencies summary

2. tests/fixtures/FIXTURES_MANIFEST.md
   - Detailed specification of each fixture
   - Schema tables (columns, types, ranges)
   - Data characteristics and use cases
   - File size estimates
   - CRS information (UTM Zone 13N)
   - Fixture validation examples
   - Complete file structure diagram

3. tests/fixtures/FIXTURE_GENERATION.md
   - Step-by-step generation guide
   - Quick start section
   - Individual fixture generation details
   - Docker-based generation instructions
   - Fixture locations and copy procedures
   - Validation procedures
   - Additional notes on data characteristics

4. tests/fixtures/CREATED_FILES.txt
   - This file
   - Inventory of all created files

GENERATOR SCRIPTS (6 files)
===========================

Excel Fixture Generators:

5. tests/fixtures/excel/generate_test_xlsx.py
   - Creates PLS_collars.xlsx (10 drill collar records)
   - Uses openpyxl
   - Generates XLS-24-01 through XLS-24-10
   - UTM Zone 13N coordinates
   - 2024 drill dates
   - Ready to run: python generate_test_xlsx.py

6. tests/fixtures/inline_generate_xlsx.py
   - Same as above, located in fixtures root
   - Can be called directly from master generator

SEG-Y Fixture Generators:

7. tests/fixtures/seismic/generate_test_segy.py
   - Creates test_2D_line.sgy (100 traces, 500 samples each)
   - Uses segyio and numpy
   - 2 ms sample interval, 1 second record
   - Synthetic sine wave (50 Hz) + noise
   - Ready to run: python generate_test_segy.py

8. tests/fixtures/inline_generate_segy.py
   - Same as above, located in fixtures root
   - Can be called directly from master generator

XYZ Fixture Generators:

9. tests/fixtures/xyz/generate_test_xyz.py
   - Creates PLS_magnetics.xyz (200 data points)
   - No external dependencies (standard library only)
   - 4 flight lines (1010, 1020, 1030, 1040)
   - 50 points per line
   - Geosoft XYZ format with comment header
   - Ready to run: python generate_test_xyz.py

10. tests/fixtures/inline_generate_xyz.py
    - Same as above, located in fixtures root
    - Can be called directly from master generator

Master Generator:

11. tests/fixtures/generate_all_fixtures_v2.py
    - Generates all three fixtures (Excel, SEG-Y, XYZ)
    - Includes dependency checking with helpful error messages
    - Creates directories if needed
    - Reports file sizes and validation
    - Error handling per fixture
    - Recommended method: python generate_all_fixtures_v2.py

TEST FIXTURES (3 files)
======================

Excel Fixture:

12. tests/fixtures/excel/PLS_collars.xlsx
    - STATUS: Ready to generate (run generator scripts)
    - 10 drill collar records
    - Columns: HoleID, Easting, Northing, Elevation, TotalDepth, Azimuth, Dip, HoleType, DrillDate, Status
    - Size: ~6 KB (estimated)
    - HoleID range: XLS-24-01 to XLS-24-10
    - Coordinates: UTM Zone 13N, Athabasca Basin
    - Drill dates: 2024-01-15 to 2024-08-15

SEG-Y Fixture:

13. tests/fixtures/seismic/test_2D_line.sgy
    - STATUS: Ready to generate (run generator scripts)
    - 100 traces, 500 samples per trace
    - 2 ms sample interval (2000 µs)
    - 1 second record length
    - Synthetic seismic data (50 Hz sine + noise)
    - Size: ~500 KB (estimated)
    - CRS: UTM Zone 13N, Athabasca Basin

XYZ Fixture:

14. tests/fixtures/xyz/PLS_magnetics.xyz
    - STATUS: CREATED (text fixture)
    - 4 flight lines (1010, 1020, 1030, 1040)
    - 200 data points total (50 per line)
    - Geosoft XYZ format with comment header
    - Columns: X, Y, LINE, MAG_TMI, MAG_RESID, ALT_RADAR
    - Size: 18 KB
    - File size verified: 18,816 bytes
    - Lines verified: 206 (6 header + 200 data)
    - Ready to use immediately

HELPER SCRIPTS (2 files)
=======================

15. tests/fixtures/copy_to_dagster.sh
    - Bash script to copy generated fixtures to Dagster test directory
    - Creates directory structure: src/dagster/tests/fixtures/{excel,seismic,xyz}
    - Copies all three fixtures
    - Usage: bash copy_to_dagster.sh

16. tests/fixtures/generate_all_fixtures.py
    - Legacy version of master generator
    - Alternative method using subprocess
    - Can be used as alternative to v2 version

  /home/Development/GeoRAG Intelligence V.1.0/tests/fixtures/copy_to_dagster.sh
  /home/Development/GeoRAG Intelligence V.1.0/tests/fixtures/run_generators.sh

NEXT STEPS
==========

1. Generate binary fixtures:
   $ cd /home/Development/GeoRAG Intelligence V.1.0
   $ python tests/fixtures/generate_all_fixtures_v2.py

2. Verify generation:
   $ ls -lh tests/fixtures/excel/PLS_collars.xlsx
   $ ls -lh tests/fixtures/seismic/test_2D_line.sgy
   $ ls -lh tests/fixtures/xyz/PLS_magnetics.xyz

3. Copy to Dagster fixtures (optional):
   $ bash tests/fixtures/copy_to_dagster.sh

4. Or copy manually:
   $ mkdir -p src/dagster/tests/fixtures/{excel,seismic,xyz}
   $ cp tests/fixtures/excel/PLS_collars.xlsx src/dagster/tests/fixtures/excel/
   $ cp tests/fixtures/seismic/test_2D_line.sgy src/dagster/tests/fixtures/seismic/
   $ cp tests/fixtures/xyz/PLS_magnetics.xyz src/dagster/tests/fixtures/xyz/

DEPENDENCIES
============

Required packages (install with pip):
  - openpyxl (for Excel fixture generation)
  - segyio (for SEG-Y fixture generation)
  - numpy (for SEG-Y fixture generation)

Install all:
  $ pip install openpyxl segyio numpy

Or per fixture:
  - Excel only: pip install openpyxl
  - SEG-Y only: pip install segyio numpy
  - XYZ only: no dependencies (text file)

DOCKER GENERATION (Alternative)
================================

Generate all fixtures inside Docker:

$ docker run --rm -v "$(pwd):/work" -w /work/tests/fixtures \
  python:3.13-slim sh -c \
  "pip install -q openpyxl segyio numpy && python generate_all_fixtures_v2.py"

NOTES
=====

- All generators are idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- XYZ fixture is already created as a text file (18 KB)
- Excel and SEG-Y fixtures need to be generated using the scripts
- All fixtures use realistic Athabasca Basin coordinates (UTM Zone 13N)
- Hole IDs use XLS-24- prefix to avoid collision with existing PLS data
- No proprietary geological data is used
- All synthetic values are within realistic geological ranges

REFERENCES
==========

- Architecture doc Section 04b: Data ingestion pipeline
- Architecture doc Section 04e: Schema definitions
- src/dagster/georag_dagster/assets/bronze_*.py: Consumer assets
- src/dagster/georag_dagster/assets/silver_*.py: Transformation assets
